verses.text: The priests seek to take Him 'by craft,' but not on the feast. Plotting. Even their scheming serves the appointed hour.
The priests seek to take Him 'by craft,' but not on the feast. Plotting. Even their scheming serves the appointed hour.
A woman breaks an alabaster box of precious ointment on His head; some grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where He sees devotion.
A woman breaks an alabaster box of precious ointment on His head; some grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where He sees devotion.
A woman breaks an alabaster box of precious ointment on His head; some grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where He sees devotion.
'She hath done what she could,' anointing Me for burial; this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
'She hath done what she could,' anointing Me for burial; this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
'She hath done what she could,' anointing Me for burial; this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
'She hath done what she could,' anointing Me for burial; this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
Judas goes to the priests to betray Him, and they promise him money. Sold for silver. The traitor bargains away his Lord.
Judas goes to the priests to betray Him, and they promise him money. Sold for silver. The traitor bargains away his Lord.
The disciples prepare the passover as He directed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
The disciples prepare the passover as He directed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
The disciples prepare the passover as He directed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
The disciples prepare the passover as He directed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
The disciples prepare the passover as He directed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
'One of you shall betray me'; woe to that man, better had he never been born. Searching warning. The traitor's doom is solemn beyond words.
'One of you shall betray me'; woe to that man, better had he never been born. Searching warning. The traitor's doom is solemn beyond words.
'One of you shall betray me'; woe to that man, better had he never been born. Searching warning. The traitor's doom is solemn beyond words.
'One of you shall betray me'; woe to that man, better had he never been born. Searching warning. The traitor's doom is solemn beyond words.
'One of you shall betray me'; woe to that man, better had he never been born. Searching warning. The traitor's doom is solemn beyond words.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
After a hymn they go out; all shall be offended, but 'after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.' Hope beyond. Even foretelling their fall, He promises to meet them again.
After a hymn they go out; all shall be offended, but 'after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.' Hope beyond. Even foretelling their fall, He promises to meet them again.
After a hymn they go out; all shall be offended, but 'after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.' Hope beyond. Even foretelling their fall, He promises to meet them again.
Peter vows he never will deny Him; 'this... night... thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. He trusts his own strength and will fall.
Peter vows he never will deny Him; 'this... night... thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. He trusts his own strength and will fall.
Peter vows he never will deny Him; 'this... night... thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. He trusts his own strength and will fall.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful unto death'; tarry and watch. Deep anguish. He bears a sorrow none can fully know.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful unto death'; tarry and watch. Deep anguish. He bears a sorrow none can fully know.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful unto death'; tarry and watch. Deep anguish. He bears a sorrow none can fully know.
He prays, 'Abba, Father... take away this cup... nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
He prays, 'Abba, Father... take away this cup... nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
Finding them asleep, 'watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation; the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Gentle rebuke. He pities their weakness even in His grief.
Finding them asleep, 'watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation; the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Gentle rebuke. He pities their weakness even in His grief.
Finding them asleep, 'watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation; the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Gentle rebuke. He pities their weakness even in His grief.
Finding them asleep, 'watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation; the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Gentle rebuke. He pities their weakness even in His grief.
Sleep on now; the hour is come; the betrayer is at hand. The hour arrives. He goes calmly to meet His suffering.
Sleep on now; the hour is come; the betrayer is at hand. The hour arrives. He goes calmly to meet His suffering.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss, the sign of betrayal; they take Him. Treacherous kiss. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss, the sign of betrayal; they take Him. Treacherous kiss. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss, the sign of betrayal; they take Him. Treacherous kiss. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss, the sign of betrayal; they take Him. Treacherous kiss. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
One draws a sword; am I a thief, that ye come with swords? the scriptures must be fulfilled. Willing surrender. He submits that the word may be accomplished.
One draws a sword; am I a thief, that ye come with swords? the scriptures must be fulfilled. Willing surrender. He submits that the word may be accomplished.
One draws a sword; am I a thief, that ye come with swords? the scriptures must be fulfilled. Willing surrender. He submits that the word may be accomplished.
All forsake Him and flee; a young man flees naked, leaving his cloth. Abandoned. In His hour the disciples scatter.
All forsake Him and flee; a young man flees naked, leaving his cloth. Abandoned. In His hour the disciples scatter.
All forsake Him and flee; a young man flees naked, leaving his cloth. Abandoned. In His hour the disciples scatter.
They lead Him to the high priest; Peter follows afar off. Distant following. To follow at a distance is the first step toward denial.
They lead Him to the high priest; Peter follows afar off. Distant following. To follow at a distance is the first step toward denial.
The council seeks witness but finds none agreeing; false witnesses rise. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
The council seeks witness but finds none agreeing; false witnesses rise. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
The council seeks witness but finds none agreeing; false witnesses rise. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
The council seeks witness but finds none agreeing; false witnesses rise. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
The council seeks witness but finds none agreeing; false witnesses rise. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
Art thou the Christ? 'I am'; ye shall see the Son of man at the right hand of power. Bold confession. He owns His glory even before His judges.
Art thou the Christ? 'I am'; ye shall see the Son of man at the right hand of power. Bold confession. He owns His glory even before His judges.
Art thou the Christ? 'I am'; ye shall see the Son of man at the right hand of power. Bold confession. He owns His glory even before His judges.
The high priest cries blasphemy; they condemn Him, spit on Him, and buffet Him. Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
The high priest cries blasphemy; they condemn Him, spit on Him, and buffet Him. Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
The high priest cries blasphemy; they condemn Him, spit on Him, and buffet Him. Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
A maid charges Peter; he denies, 'I know not what thou sayest.' First fall. The bold disciple begins to slide.
A maid charges Peter; he denies, 'I know not what thou sayest.' First fall. The bold disciple begins to slide.
A maid charges Peter; he denies, 'I know not what thou sayest.' First fall. The bold disciple begins to slide.
Charged again, he denies it; the bystanders say, 'Surely thou art a Galilaean.' Deeper denial. Each step sinks him further.
Charged again, he denies it; the bystanders say, 'Surely thou art a Galilaean.' Deeper denial. Each step sinks him further.
He curses and swears he knows not the man; the cock crows, and he weeps. Bitter tears. The fall is grievous, but the grief is real.
He curses and swears he knows not the man; the cock crows, and he weeps. Bitter tears. The fall is grievous, but the grief is real.
